# nsec-tree-cli

> Nostr users are stuck with one key per identity. Separating contexts (personal, work, anonymous) means managing unrelated keys with no structured recovery and no way to prove they're connected. nsec-tree-cli is an offline-first CLI that derives unlimited unlinkable Nostr identities from a single root secret, with optional cryptographic ownership proofs and human-friendly Shamir backup.

## Install / run

```bash
# One-off via npx (no install required)
npx nsec-tree-cli root create --name main

# Or install globally
npm install -g nsec-tree-cli
nsec-tree root create --name main
```

Node >= 22 required. ESM-only.

## Key concepts

- **Root** — a starting secret (mnemonic-backed or nsec-backed) from which all identities derive deterministically. Mnemonic-backed roots support phrase recovery and Shamir splitting; nsec-backed roots derive the same tree but cannot be phrase-recovered.
- **Derivation path** — a slash-separated sequence of named segments (`personal/forum-burner@0`). Same root + same path = same identity, always. Path segments: lowercase, shell-friendly, max 32 chars, optional `@index` suffix (defaults to `@0`).
- **Child identity** — a standalone Nostr keypair (npub/nsec) derived from the root. Unlinkable to other children by default.
- **Ownership proof** — a cryptographic proof that a child shares a root. Private proofs hide the derivation path; full proofs reveal it.
- **Shamir splitting** — splits a mnemonic into N shares where any T reconstruct it. Word-encoded via `@forgesworn/shamir-words`.
- **Profile** — a named saved root stored in `~/.nsec-tree/profiles/` (mode 0o600). One profile is active at a time.

---

## Global options (all commands)

| Flag | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `--json` | Machine-readable JSON output |
| `--quiet` | Bare values only, no formatting or hints |
| `--no-hints` | Suppress contextual "Try next" suggestions |
| `--help` | Show usage text |

Environment variables:
- `NSEC_TREE_NO_HINTS=1` — permanently disable hints
- `NO_COLOR=1` — disable colour output
- `NSEC_TREE_MNEMONIC=<phrase>` — root input from environment
- `NSEC_TREE_NSEC=<nsec>` — root input from environment
- `NSEC_TREE_PROFILE=<name>` — active profile override

Root input sources (mutually exclusive): `--mnemonic`, `--nsec`, `--root-file`, `--stdin`, `--profile`. When none are provided, the active profile is used automatically.

---

## Command reference

### `root create`

Generate a new BIP-39 mnemonic-backed root.

```
nsec-tree root create [--passphrase <text>] [--name <profile>] [--json] [--quiet] [--out <file>]
```

```bash
nsec-tree root create --name main
# Generates 12-word mnemonic, saves as profile 'main', sets it as active

nsec-tree root create --name work --out ~/work-root.json
# Also saves root descriptor to a file (mode 0o600)

nsec-tree root create --json
# → { "rootType": "mnemonic-backed", "recoverable": true,
#     "masterNpub": "npub1...", "mnemonic": "word1 word2 ..." }
```

---

### `root restore`

Restore an existing mnemonic-backed root.

```
nsec-tree root restore (--mnemonic <phrase> | --root-file <file> | --stdin) [--passphrase <text>] [--name <profile>] [--json] [--quiet] [--out <file>]
```

```bash
nsec-tree root restore --mnemonic "word1 word2 ... word12" --name main
nsec-tree root restore --stdin < backup.json --name recovered
```

---

### `root import-nsec`

Import an existing nsec as a root (nsec-backed root — cannot be phrase-recovered, but supports full derivation).

```
nsec-tree root import-nsec (--nsec <nsec> | --root-file <file> | --stdin) [--name <profile>] [--json] [--quiet] [--out <file>]
```

---

### `root inspect`

Show metadata about a root (master npub, root type, recoverability).

```
nsec-tree root inspect [(--mnemonic | --nsec | --root-file | --stdin | --profile <name>)] [--json]
```

```bash
nsec-tree root inspect --json
# → { "rootType": "mnemonic-backed", "recoverable": true, "masterNpub": "npub1..." }
```

---

### `derive path`

Derive a child identity at a hierarchical path.

```
nsec-tree derive path <path> [(root input options)] [--passphrase <text>] [--json]
```

```bash
nsec-tree derive path personal/forum-burner
nsec-tree derive path work/github@0 --json
# → { "rootType": "mnemonic-backed", "path": "work/github@0",
#     "segments": [...], "npub": "npub1...", "nsec": "nsec1...",
#     "publicKey": "<hex>", "purpose": "forum-burner", "index": 0 }
```

---

### `derive persona`

Derive a single top-level named persona identity.

```
nsec-tree derive persona <name> [(root input options)] [--passphrase <text>] [--json]
```

```bash
nsec-tree derive persona anonymous
```

---

### `derive account`

Alias for `derive path` — derive a child at an arbitrary path.

```
nsec-tree derive account <path> [(root input options)] [--passphrase <text>] [--json]
```

---

### `export npub`

Export just the public key (npub) for a derived identity.

```
nsec-tree export npub <path> [(root input options)] [--passphrase <text>] [--json] [--quiet]
```

```bash
nsec-tree export npub personal/forum-burner --quiet
# → npub1abc...

nsec-tree export npub personal/forum-burner --json
# → { "path": "personal/forum-burner@0", "npub": "npub1..." }
```

---

### `export nsec`

Export the private key (nsec) for a derived identity. Output is flagged as sensitive.

```
nsec-tree export nsec <path> [(root input options)] [--passphrase <text>] [--json] [--quiet] [--out <file>]
```

```bash
nsec-tree export nsec personal/forum-burner --quiet
# → nsec1abc...

nsec-tree export nsec personal/forum-burner --out ~/forum-burner.nsec
# Writes to file with mode 0o600

nsec-tree export nsec personal/forum-burner --json
# → { "path": "personal/forum-burner@0", "nsec": "nsec1..." }
```

---

### `export identity`

Export the full identity bundle (npub + nsec + metadata).

```
nsec-tree export identity <path> [(root input options)] [--passphrase <text>] [--json] [--out <file>]
```

```bash
nsec-tree export identity personal/forum-burner --json
# → { "path": "personal/forum-burner@0", "npub": "npub1...", "nsec": "nsec1...",
#     "publicKey": "<hex>", "purpose": "forum-burner", "index": 0 }
```

---

### `prove private`

Generate a private ownership proof — proves a child identity shares a root without revealing the derivation path.

```
nsec-tree prove private <path> [(root input options)] [--passphrase <text>] [--json] [--out <file>]
```

```bash
nsec-tree prove private personal/forum-burner --out proof.json

nsec-tree prove private personal/forum-burner --json
# → { "masterPubkey": "<hex>", "childPubkey": "<hex>",
#     "attestation": "...", "signature": "..." }
```

---

### `prove full`

Generate a full ownership proof — proves shared root and reveals the derivation path.

```
nsec-tree prove full <path> [(root input options)] [--passphrase <text>] [--json] [--out <file>]
```

---

### `verify proof`

Verify a proof file (private or full).

```
nsec-tree verify proof [<file> | --stdin] [--json] [--quiet]
```

```bash
nsec-tree verify proof proof.json
nsec-tree verify proof --stdin < proof.json --json
# → { "valid": true, "proofType": "private",
#     "proof": { "masterPubkey": "...", "childPubkey": "..." } }
```

---

### `shamir split`

Split a mnemonic-backed root into N Shamir shares (any T reconstruct it). Shares are BIP-39 word-encoded.

```
nsec-tree shamir split [(root input options)] --shares <n> --threshold <t> [--json] [--out-dir <dir>]
```

```bash
nsec-tree shamir split --shares 5 --threshold 3
# Generates 5 share files, any 3 reconstruct the mnemonic

nsec-tree shamir split --shares 3 --threshold 2 --out-dir ~/shamir-backup
# Writes share files to the specified directory (mode 0o700)
```

---

### `shamir recover`

Reconstruct a mnemonic from T or more Shamir shares.

```
nsec-tree shamir recover [<share-file> ... | --stdin] [--json] [--out <file>]
```

```bash
nsec-tree shamir recover share-1.json share-2.json share-3.json
nsec-tree shamir recover share-1.json share-2.json --json
# → { "rootType": "mnemonic-backed", "mnemonic": "word1 word2 ...", "recoverable": true }
```

---

### `profile save`

Save a root as a named profile in `~/.nsec-tree/profiles/`.

```
nsec-tree profile save <name> (--mnemonic | --nsec | --root-file | --stdin) [--passphrase <text>] [--force] [--use] [--json]
```

```bash
nsec-tree profile save work --mnemonic "word1 ..." --use
# --use sets 'work' as the active profile
# --force overwrites if name already exists
```

---

### `profile list`

List all saved profiles.

```
nsec-tree profile list [--json]
```

```bash
nsec-tree profile list --json
# → [{ "name": "main", "active": true, "rootType": "mnemonic-backed", "recoverable": true }, ...]
```

---

### `profile use`

Set the active profile.

```
nsec-tree profile use <name> [--json]
```

---

### `profile show`

Show details of a named profile (or the active one if no name given).

```
nsec-tree profile show [name] [--json]
```

---

### `profile remove`

Remove a saved profile.

```
nsec-tree profile remove <name> [--json]
```

---

### `inspect path`

Examine a derivation path — parse and describe its segments without performing derivation.

```
nsec-tree inspect path <path> [--json]
```

```bash
nsec-tree inspect path personal/forum-burner@2 --json
# → { "segments": [{ "name": "personal", "requestedIndex": 0 },
#                  { "name": "forum-burner", "requestedIndex": 2 }] }
```

---

### `inspect root`

Inspect a root without persisting it. Shows metadata.

```
nsec-tree inspect root [(root input options)] [--json]
```

---

### `explain`

Built-in tutorials rendered in the terminal.

```
nsec-tree explain model|proofs|recovery|paths|offline
```

| Topic | Content |
|-------|---------|
| `model` | How hierarchical derivation works |
| `proofs` | How private and full ownership proofs work |
| `recovery` | Mnemonic and Shamir recovery options |
| `paths` | Path syntax, segments, and index semantics |
| `offline` | Air-gapped usage and security model |

---

## JSON output shapes

All `--json` output is stable across patch versions.

```typescript
// root create / root restore / root import-nsec
{ rootType: 'mnemonic-backed' | 'nsec-backed', recoverable: boolean,
  masterNpub: string, mnemonic?: string }

// derive path / derive persona / derive account
{ rootType: string, path: string,
  segments: Array<{ name: string, requestedIndex: number, actualIndex: number, npub: string }>,
  npub: string, nsec: string, publicKey: string, purpose: string, index: number }

// export npub
{ path: string, npub: string }

// export nsec
{ path: string, nsec: string }

// export identity
{ path: string, npub: string, nsec: string, publicKey: string, purpose: string, index: number }

// prove private
{ masterPubkey: string, childPubkey: string, attestation: string, signature: string }

// prove full
{ masterPubkey: string, childPubkey: string, path: string, attestation: string, signature: string }

// verify proof
{ valid: boolean, proofType: 'private' | 'full', proof: object }

// shamir split
{ shares: string[], threshold: number, total: number }

// shamir recover
{ rootType: string, mnemonic: string, recoverable: boolean }

// profile list
Array<{ name: string, active: boolean, rootType: string, recoverable: boolean }>
```

---

## Security model

- All crypto operations are handled by the `nsec-tree` library — the CLI owns grammar, I/O, and formatting only.
- The root secret never touches a network. All derivation is local and offline.
- Secret files are written with mode `0o600`; directories with `0o700`.
- `NSEC_TREE_MNEMONIC` and `NSEC_TREE_NSEC` environment variables are accepted but not logged.
- Shamir shares are individually harmless below the threshold; combine only on a trusted offline machine.

---

## Built on

- [nsec-tree](https://www.npmjs.com/package/nsec-tree) — derivation, proofs, and verification library
- [@forgesworn/shamir-words](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@forgesworn/shamir-words) — Shamir secret sharing with BIP-39 word output
- [@scure/bip39](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@scure/bip39) — mnemonic generation and validation

---

- [ARCHITECTURE.md](https://github.com/forgesworn/nsec-tree-cli/blob/main/ARCHITECTURE.md) — separation of concerns between CLI and libraries
